Petition For Confirmation Of Sale Of Personal Property
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Continued To Aug-20-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lilly Moallem for Nicholas Donohoe (Executor).

The Court inquired about there being an operation agreement which would set forth any buyout information. In addition, the Court expressed its concerns with the lack of information regarding the breakdown of the capital contributions and the seller's representations & warranties with regard to the sales agreement. In addition, the Court stated that it did not know if the purchaser had also been the managing partner. Ms. Moallen informed the Court that the partnership agreement is attached to the petition, stated that they inherited this matter from a previous attorney, and that as far as they know there was no buy/sell agreement. The Court presented additional concerns with regard to the petition noting that the Estate should not be left with any liabilities. The Court further stated that it did not know dates or what the rents were which were which cause for some concern. Ms. Moallen stated that this was an asset that the decedent inherited from his Aunt, that about one hundred days had passed since the petition was filed, that the purchaser is getting a little nervous, and that all the beneficiaries support the sale. The matter was continued to August 20, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Moallem is to prepare a declaration addressing the Court's concerns and file it by August 15, 2013. Ms. Moallen's declaration should address whether or not there is an operating agreement/buyout provision, breakdown of what the capital contributions were, and address the sales agreement with regard to the seller's representations & warranties.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Urbanski for Jeffrey Brooks (Respondent). Lily Moallen for Nicholas Donohoe.

Ms. Moallem informed the Court that an inventory for DMC (Donohoe Management Company) was on partial inventory #4 filed on September 27, 2012 and then corrected on February 6, 2013. The Court stated that it does not have access to the LOR management assets/records, that it is only provided with income and that it will interlineate in the order that "The Court is not making any determination as to the assets or actions of LOR as the Estate only holds a limited partner interest in the patnership". Ms. Moallem asked that the Court treat those assets (management co.) as Estate assets, stated that she has not received the receipt on distribution from Ms. Murray and that she will file copies of the cancelled check. In addition, Ms. Loallem addressed Mr. Urbanski's concerns regarding the tax liability as well as overlap in fees by stating that she is working with the Accountant to amend the 706, that distribution was made to decrease taxes on the Estate, and that the Accountant's fees are for preparation of the income taxes, the estate, DMC, Cunningham, Donohoe Investments and Linos Ranch. The Accounting Petition and the Petition for Extraordinary Compensation were taken under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-12-2013 Continued To Mar-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lemoine Skinner, III for Anita Donohoe (Objector). Lily Moallem and Peter Myers for and with Nicholas Donohoe (Petitioner/Executor). Yulissa Zulaica for Jeffrey Brooks (Respondent).

Mr. Skinner stated that he fine with the Court moving his petition for attorney's fees from March 27, 2013 to May 7, 2013. Ms. Moallem stated that all appraisals have been filed, and stated that the $150.00 per hour compensation to Mr. Donohoe was the average professional fiduciary fee for executors. The Court stated that Mr. Donohoe is not a professional fiduciary, that the normal rate that is allowed by the Court is $40 per hour, that it was inclined to defer any requests for extraordinary compensation to Mr. Donohoe until final distribution and that it was appropriate to compensate a portion of extraordinary fees for Mr. Myers but not all. Ms. Moallem spoke of how the LLC is managed, explained that there is a holding account for rents collected on the medical building from which expenses are paid and distribution is made to the estate, that the Napa property agreement only addresses the monthly mortgage payments but not the property tax which have been historically split and that the Estate is responsible for half of those. Ms. Moallem also addressed the accountant/bookkeeping fees. Mr. Skinner objected to the extraordinary fees for litigation of the creditors claim, raised the issue of conflict of interest with regard to Nicholas' wife being the sales broker, objected to extraordinary fees for the executor and the executor's attorney for sale of the property and stated that ordinary fees should be supported by time records. Ms. Zulaica asked the Court to be conservative when awarding fees. Mr. Myers explained circumstances/time spent to support his fee request. The Accounting Petition as well as the Petition for Allowance of Extraordinary Fees were continued to March 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of receipts on distribution. Both matters will be deemed under submission on that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chen.
